text.title: vr-design-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Open-space scene without horizon or ground plane; first-minute nausea reports widespread.
antipattern.root-cause: Artistic choice for 'infinite space' aesthetic; vestibular anchor removed.
antipattern.fix: Add subtle ground plane + horizon line; even faint cues anchor the vestibular system.

antipattern.symptom: Smooth locomotion forced on every user; 30% abandonment by minute 5.
antipattern.fix: Set teleport as novice default; offer smooth as opt-in with comfort warning.

antipattern.symptom: Health/score HUD locked to head; store review rejects.
antipattern.fix: Move to body-anchored wrist menu or world-anchored overlay; reserve head-locked for safety only.

antipattern.symptom: User in wheelchair cannot reach objects; complaints filed.
antipattern.fix: Add seated toggle; reposition interactables to seated reach.
